news.title: BuzzWorks forays in Mysuru with 16,000 sq ft office space
news.para: BuzzWorks, Brigade Group's managed office and co-working division, has launched a 16,000 sq ft center in Mysuru, offering over 500 seats. This expansion is part of a broader multi-city growth strategy for the financial year, with further developments planned in Bengaluru and a new entry into Chennai.

news.title: India’s average residential rents rise 14% in Jan-Mar 2026: Magicbricks
news.para: India’s residential rental market saw average rents rise 14% year-on-year and 2% sequentially in January-March 2026, according to Magicbricks. While demand grew marginally, supply increased at a faster pace, led by new completions and handovers in peripheral markets across major cities.

news.title: Grade-A office absorption rises 5% to 13.5 million sq ft in Q1 2026: Anarock
news.para: Net absorption of Grade A office space across the top seven cities rose five per cent year-on-year to 13.5 million sq ft in Q1 2026, driven by Bengaluru, Hyderabad and Chennai, according to Anarock. Overall vacancy declined to 15.5 per cent from 16.3 per cent a year earlier, while GCCs continued to account for a rising share of office leasing demand.

news.title: Urban Vault expects fit-out & facility management business to generate ₹50 crore revenue in FY27
news.para: Urban Vault anticipates ₹50 crore revenue from its design, fit-out, and facility management services by FY27, driven by corporate outsourcing trends. The company recently secured a ₹100 crore contract and projects over ₹200 crore turnover in FY26 with significant year-on-year growth.

news.title: Kolkata metro's orange line extension till sector V likely by December 2026
news.para: Kolkata Metro Railway is set to extend its Orange Line services to Sector V by December 2026. Essential viaduct work at the Chingrighata crossing begins this Friday. This will bridge a critical gap, facilitating the much-needed connection between southern suburbs and Sector V. Permissions have been granted for traffic blocks to complete this vital project.

news.title: Nisus Finance invests ₹90 crore in Realnet Ventures' project in Pune's Hinjewadi
news.para: Nisus Finance Services has injected ₹90 crore into Realnet Ventures, a Paranjape Schemes subsidiary, for a residential project in Pune's Blue Ridge township. This investment targets an 188-unit apartment tower with 3BHK and 4BHK configurations, boasting a significant saleable area and a projected gross development value of ₹370 crore. The development is situated within a 150-acre mixed-use township.

news.title: Maharashtra approves handover of three plots for Mula-Mutha riverfront development project
news.para: The Maharashtra state government has approved the transfer of three land plots totaling 52,851 sqm for Pune's riverfront development project. The Standing Committee also greenlit extending the project from Mundhwa to Manjari and sanctioned land for a garbage transfer unit. Additionally, a heritage conservation project for the Peshwa-era water supply system was approved.

news.title: Mantra Group elevates Rohit Gupta as MD; Trilegal appoints Sandeep Ghosh as CEO
news.para: Mantra Group has appointed Rohit Gupta as managing director and Saransh Dey as CEO for Mumbai. Trilegal has named Sandeep Ghosh as its new CEO. Ghosh will focus on strategic priorities and client relationships. Trilegal also recently opened a new office in Chennai, expanding its presence to nine offices across India.

news.title: Lucknow civic body plans to bring in OTS scheme for tax dues
news.para: Lucknow Municipal Corporation is set to launch a one-time settlement scheme to address Rs 700 crore in pending house tax and Rs 110 crore in water/sewerage dues. This initiative aims to provide relief to nearly 2.5 lakh house owners by potentially waiving interest and surcharges, allowing them to clear principal amounts and boosting civic body revenue.

news.title: Map approval to be must for rural multi-storey buildings in Bihar
news.para: In a progressive move, Bihar plans to enforce map approvals for high-rise buildings in rural locales, aimed at controlling chaotic urbanization. A newly established regulatory body, akin to Rera, will manage construction activities, compelling builders to obtain authorized plans, place funds in escrow, and reveal comprehensive project information.

news.title: NDMC announces 50% parking rebate for EV in Lutyens' Delhi
news.para: The New Delhi Municipal Council (NDMC) is promoting green transport with a 50% parking rebate for electric vehicles and new shuttle bus services for employees. These initiatives aim to encourage sustainable development, fuel conservation, and environmental protection, aligning with national goals.

news.title: Flat-owners in Odisha to pay ₹50,000 stamp fee for common area registration in apartments
news.para: Odisha has streamlined apartment registrations by amending the Indian Stamp Act, significantly reducing the financial burden on apartment owners' associations. A nominal Rs 50,000 stamp duty now applies for transferring common areas, replacing a previous 5% charge. This move aims to resolve registration bottlenecks and ease the use of shared facilities for residents.

news.title: Illegal walls, rooms in offices and housing complexes face the hammer in Gurugram
news.para: Gurugram's enforcement wing conducted a sealing and demolition drive against unauthorized structures, including an internal wall, a vend, and a servant room. The action followed complaints and due legal procedures, targeting violations in residential and commercial properties. Authorities emphasized the ongoing nature of such drives to curb illegal constructions.

news.title: Experts, homebuyers welcome replacement of jail term provision in RERA Act
news.para: Real estate experts and homebuyers in Odisha have welcomed the Centre's decision to replace imprisonment with monetary penalties for allottees non-compliance with RERA tribunal orders. This move aims to decriminalize business laws and reduce court burdens, making the process more pragmatic and less intimidating for buyers.

news.title: Ghaziabad: Owners of charred Green Avenue flats seek FIR against RWA after condo fire
news.para: Residents of Gaur Green Avenue are considering legal action against their welfare association following a major fire. They allege that unauthorized constructions blocked emergency access and fire safety systems failed. An inquiry report supports these claims, noting hampered rescue operations. Affected families are demanding investigations and compensation for significant losses.

news.title: Hyderabad-based builder in soup for mortgaging villa without property owner’s consent
news.para: A villa owner in Hyderabad has filed a police complaint alleging her property was illegally mortgaged by the builder in October 2025 to a corporate firm as unsold property. The builder allegedly mortgaged several other properties, including a senior citizen's venture, to secure loans. Police have registered a case against the real estate company and the lending firm.

news.title: Uber leases over nine lakh sq ft Hyderabad office through 10-year lease for GCC
news.para: Uber India Systems has secured a massive office space in Hyderabad. The company will occupy over 9 lakh square feet for a decade. This deal is valued at nearly Rs 839 crore. It highlights Uber's growing commitment to India. Hyderabad continues to attract global companies for technology and operations.

news.title: HCC's net profit drops 34.56% to ₹59 crore in Q4 FY26
news.para: Hindustan Construction Company (HCC) reported a 34.56% decline in consolidated net profit to Rs 59 crore for the quarter ended March 2026, despite a full-year profit increase to Rs 165.52 crore. The company secured Rs 4,554 crore in new orders during FY26 and maintains a robust order book of Rs 12,971 crore.

news.title: Jharkhand launches online portal to regularise unauthorised buildings
news.para: Jharkhand Urban Development Minister Sudivya Kumar launched an online portal for regularizing unauthorized residential buildings, following the state cabinet's approval of new rules. This initiative aims to provide relief to homeowners with un-sanctioned plans, simplifying the process and allowing fee payments in installments.

news.title: Himachal Pradesh chief secretary seeks reports on RERA 'irregularities' in 15 days
news.para: Himachal Pradesh Chief Secretary Sanjay Gupta has ordered the State Vigilance and Anti-Corruption Bureau to investigate real estate projects. The probe focuses on Joint Development Agreements and potential violations of land reform laws. The RERA has been asked to provide relevant documents within 15 days. This action aims to uncover irregularities in land agreements between local farmers and non-agriculturists.
